submission.questionnaireText:
Submitted by Melinda Poling through Client Portal.

Therapy for:
Individual
Gender:
Woman (cisgender)
State:
OR
Contact method:
Contact form
Client message:
I’m in a desperate situation with ongoing bladder and kidney pain. My attempts to fix it help some and not enough. My heart has been pounding, my system wake in panic and stick with me for several hours. I’ve been dealing with an overgrowth of bacteria in my gut for over 30 years. Several of my organs seem to be affected. My oldest son is going through your program now. We have a lot of similar issues. I’ve been working at my diet and taking natural fighters (herbs, colloidal silver, garlic etc) as well as builders probiotics all with varying results. A recent antibiotic for a tooth infection took all my symptoms away for 2 weeks but whatever I’m fighting came back with a vengeance. I’m ready for help.


---

Melinda Poling Poling (Melinda Poling)
Adult 07/09/1971 (54)  Schedule appointment  Edit

1. Initial Health Survey (complete at your earliest convenience)
08/21/2025 at 5:11 PM
Note



1. Do you gain weight easily?
(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me

(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me

(5) This is an extremely bothersome issue for me

Sugar, fruit, honey, stevia, carbs (bread, grains of any kind), starches of any kind (potatoes, yams or sweet potatoes, noodles, etc.)

(1) I do not experience this

(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me

(4) This is a very bothersome issue for me
49. Do symptoms alleviate after a bowel movement?
Yes
50. Have you tried anything in the past to alleviate your symptoms? These can be dietary changes, supplements, prescriptions or anything else. If so, please list them, and how helpful (or unhelpful) they were for you. Please be as specific as possible, including specific foods and brands and dosages of supplements, as well as what symptoms they affected.
Yes
Paud arco and Jatoba tea, oregano, colloidal silver, enzymes (caprylic acid),
Probiotics of strong and various kinds: Dr. Ohhira's 5 a day (fruit in this may be taking me backwards)
For toxins: Pectasol C, charcoal, cilantro, and Cellcore's Lymphactiv -and - HM-ET Binder
51. Are you currently taking any supplements? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any supplement. Please mention how helpful (or unhelpful) you perceive them to be.
Yes
Nutrients: Female Balance - PureTrim, Zinc, UroTone - Professional Formulas, zinc 30 - Pure encapsulations, GTF Chromium - Genestra 200 mcg, Biotin - Natrol, EliteOmega 3 gems (Fish Oil 32,00mg) - Carlson, D3 4,000 IU - Protocol for Life Balance, Magnesium LifeMag 1400 mg, & 600 mg Magnesium - GlycinateNootropics Depot .
Fighters on empty stomach 3 x day: colloidal silver 500ppm - Natural Path Silver Wings 2 t. 3-4x day, Paud arco tea 1 qt day (pretty dilute), also I take capsuls of pad arco 3 x day, Candicide forte Ortho Molecular Products, Defend - profesional Wellness (sodium caprylate 400 mg, olive leaf extract 400 mg)
52. Are you currently taking any medications? If so, please list them and be as specific as possible, including the brand and dosage of any medication.
Yes
Thyroid - Nature Throid 2 Grain
Fluconazole 150 mg (2 doses)
53. Are you currently diagnosed with any medical conditions? If so, please list them.
Yes
Neuropathy-tingling numb on left side of my body when stressed, particularly seasonally, winter.
Insulin Resistance–on a strict 30 gr of carbs or less a day diet, I still get blood sugar readings around 100.
54. Please describe your diet. What foods do you typically eat, and in what amounts? Are these foods prepared by you, or are they pre-packaged, or from a restaurant? At what times do you typically eat?
I prepare all my own meals - 2 a day. No snacks! Protein would look like 2 eggs and maybe a small turkey sausage or cabbage with olive oil dressing I make. Lately I've moved to lunch and a 6:00/6:30 dinner which would be often cabbage (red or green) sardines/or/ground turkey /or/ beef and Aoli–egg yolk, lemon juice, olive oil, garlic, onion, salt and oregano. Maybe a slice or 2 of cucumber and or tomatoes.

55. Please describe your lifestyle. Do you get outside often? Do you exercise? How stressful is your day-to-day life due to work or other factors?
Walk each morning a mile or so

56. What are your health primary goals you are looking achieve?
Get rid of bladder and kidney pain (reoccurring daily)

Control Insulin resistance with a wider variety of foods

57. Have you ever had food poisoning before? If so, when?
Not sure. I have an iron stomach

58. Please list your height, weight and waist measurements.
5'3" 126 lbs

waist: 32 1/2"

59. Please list any other information you think would help us help you.
I've struggled with yeast (?) issues for over 30 years. Often bladder or when I was nursing - breast infections, ongoing. I learned to get dairy out of my diet, sugar, honey and now anything sweet at all. Even as strict as I've become on my diet and dedicated to the regime that seemed at first/and at times seemed to help is no longer allowing me to succeed. The insulin resistance seems an important key. I've had A1C over 6.

I've lost 25 lbs since October and adopted a very strict diet of staying under 30 carbs a day. At a mystery of what keeps me stuck. I've wondered about parasites, but every stool sample comes back negative. I've taken an herbal tincture for parasite cleansing once with a major headache and die off. I tend to struggle with die off if I change things too fast, but my body is getting pretty used to processing something all the time. I know how to fight yeast and fungus but my cleansing system to take care of die off is pretty weak. I wonder if my kidneys are just tired of filtering out the extras. I have had a test run to detect Myco toxins. Evidently, I have a LOT in my system. I can produce a report if you'd like. I'm trying to figure out what to eat and take to overcome. I also experience cortisol spikes at times (shaking or hot/clod sweats) over the smallest issues.

submission.advisorNotesText:
Clinician: Elie Klein
Edit  
3:27 PM
More info collected today:
Good morning I have some questions: does the pain always come on an hour or two after eating? what do you het more often, kidney or bladder pain? the kidney pain is always exactly on the lower back behind the kidneys? is there a formal diagnosis?

Today 1:44 pm

As long as I eat the low carb diet and take t these herbs, I don’t have pain. But after having the smoothie I battled a dull persistent pain for 10-12 hours. The rife machine was very helpful as well as taking extra herbs/coliodal silver. If I ate like that every day the pain would be continual and would go from kidney to bladder until my whole core, stomach too, would be inflamed. Literally, inflammation - evidenced by an extended belly, hard to have a BM because of swelling, and poop would be skinny because of inflamed intestines. There also seems to be a factor that if I have sweet my body in that inflamed condition hangs onto content longer. I get kidney pain more often, at this season of my life. It used to be bladder first and only go to kidney if I didn’t get it under control. As far as a diagnosis, I don’t. My ND doesn’t like to label, likes to be super positive, I only got the GFR reading that was 52 - September of 22. I suspect because of ongoing infection at the time. I just checked and my more recent GFR readings were over 100. The rife settings that help me treat: Chronic kidney failure, bladder problems, pain relief, and pancreatic insufficiency Hope that helps.

I get the impression, I don’t have a disease, but rather some microbe that makes my organs have to work really hard to just function. Hopeful to find a plan to get it under control. Eager to hear your thoughts.

My assessment of the situation that I communicated with her:
With a low metabolic rate it is hard to support lasting healing. A ketogenic diet is not conducive to healing in the long term. It seems to me we need to support the healing with antimicrobials in the mix, other possible remedies (that I will determine once I get the new test results) as well as a gradual increase in carbohydrate intake, which is crucial to the support of metabolic rate and function, and therefore to healing. We'll take it step by step.

Clinician: Elie Klein
8:51 AM
Further review of labs:
TSH 3.2, low fT3, fT4
elevated Alfatoxin, Patullin, Ochratoxin, Env. toxins, herbicides

Consider PC, progesterone, CDO increase T3, T4 dose, iodine

Chart Note
Clinician: Elie Klein
Edit  
9:10 AM
Subjective:

Patient Information: Melinda, 54-year-old female, 5'3", 126 lbs, waist 32.5 inches.

Chief Complaints:
Recurrent daily bladder and kidney pain (rated 5/5, extremely bothersome).

Insulin resistance with desire to control it through a wider variety of foods (A1C >6).
History of Present Illness:
30-year history of suspected yeast/fungal issues (bladder infections, mastitis during nursing).

Symptoms include lower abdominal pain (5/5), brain fog, low energy, lack of mental clarity (4/5), afternoon crash after lunch (4/5), irritability (4/5), loss of pleasure in activities (4/5), low libido (4/5), constipation (4/5), and symptoms worsening post-meal (4/5).

Less bothersome symptoms: low appetite (3/5), waking during sleep (3/5), upper abdominal pain (3/5).

Dental issues (bad breath, cavities, gum inflammation, 5/5).

Recent weight loss of 25 lbs since October on a strict ketogenic diet (<30 carbs/day).

Fasting glucose 122 mg/dL despite low-carb diet; reports high triglycerides and possible fatty liver in early 20s.

Experiences cortisol spikes (shaking, hot/cold sweats) with minor stressors.

Suspects ongoing fungal issues (no thick white tongue coating); mycotoxin test indicates high levels.

Negative stool samples for parasites; previous parasite cleanse caused headache and die-off.

Sensitive to sweets, starches, and even stevia; fruit (e.g., fig) triggers pain within hours.

Past penicillin use for dental infection resolved bladder/kidney issues temporarily but symptoms returned.

Past Medical History:
Partial hysterectomy 4-5 years ago.

History of IBS with bleeding bowels (resolved with dietary changes).

No significant musculoskeletal injuries or back pain; occasional neck tension (stress-related).

Tested negative for MS; no UTI despite bladder symptoms (naturopath noted multiple bacteria).

Medications/Supplements:
Antifungals: Pau d’arco tea (1 qt/day), Jatoba tea, oregano oil, colloidal silver (500 ppm, 2 tsp 3-4x/day), caprylic acid.

Probiotics: Dr. Ohhira’s (5/day, concern about fruit content).

Detox: Pectasol-C, charcoal, cilantro, Cellcore Lymphactiv, HM-ET Binder.

Nutrients: Female Balance (PureTrim), UroTone (Professional Formulas), Zinc 30 (Pure Encapsulations), GTF Chromium (200 mcg, Genestra), Biotin (Natrol), Elite Omega-3 (3200 mg, Carlson), D3 (4000 IU, Protocol for Life Balance), Magnesium (1400 mg LifeMag, 600 mg Glycinate, Nootropics Depot).

Other: Rife machine (therapeutic, helps bladder/kidney pain temporarily).

Social History: Former teacher, high stress in early 20s (school and teaching); father is a chiropractor.

Diet: Ketogenic, 2 meals/day, <30 carbs/day (e.g., meat, cabbage); avoids dairy, wheat, sugar, honey, fruit, and starches due to symptom triggers.

Vitals Reported: Heart rate 53-64 BPM, body temperature 97-97.2°F, urination 8-11x/day, bowel movements 1-3x/day (Bristol Type 5-6).
Objective:

Physical Exam: Not performed (telehealth consultation).

Vital Signs:
Heart rate: 53-64 BPM.

Body temperature: 97-97.2°F.

Urination frequency: 8-11x/day.

Bowel movements: 1-3x/day, Bristol Type 5-6.

Labs/Tests:
A1C >6 (date not specified).

Fasting glucose: 122 mg/dL (recent).

High triglycerides and possible fatty liver noted ~30 years ago.

Mycotoxin test: High levels (report to be uploaded).

Negative stool samples for parasites.

Fasting insulin (December/January, to be provided).

No recent UTI confirmed despite bladder symptoms.

Dietary Intake: ~1475 calories/day (per Chronometer), low-carb (<30 g/day), high fat, moderate protein.
Assessment:

Chronic Fungal/Bacterial Overgrowth (Likely Candida or Mixed Infection):
Long-standing history of yeast-like symptoms (bladder pain, mastitis) exacerbated by sugars/starches.

Temporary relief with antifungals (pau d’arco, colloidal silver) and penicillin (for dental infection).

High mycotoxin levels suggest fungal burden; negative parasite tests but possible die-off reaction.

Rife machine and antifungals reduce bladder/kidney pain, indicating microbial involvement.

Insulin Resistance:
Fasting glucose 122 mg/dL and A1C >6 despite strict low-carb diet.

Possible endotoxin interference from gut bacteria affecting pancreatic function and glucose metabolism.

Low-carb diet may exacerbate cortisol spikes, contributing to insulin resistance and stress response.

Possible Estrogen Dominance:
Partial hysterectomy suggests hormonal imbalance; symptoms (irritability, low libido, brain fog) consistent with low progesterone.

Estrogen may contribute to histamine release, increasing gut sensitivity and systemic inflammation.

Fatty Liver (Historical):
High triglycerides and possible fatty liver in 20s; may contribute to current insulin resistance and detox challenges.
Kidney/Bladder Pain (Non-UTI):
Likely related to microbial overgrowth (fungal/bacterial); resolves with penicillin or antifungals but recurs.

Triggers include sugars, starches, and fruit; delayed onset (1-2 hours) suggests gut fermentation.

Other Symptoms:
Brain fog, low energy, and afternoon crash likely due to insufficient glucose (brain requires ~120 g/day).

Low libido, irritability, and loss of pleasure may reflect hormonal/stress imbalances.

Dental issues and constipation suggest systemic inflammation and sluggish detox pathways.

Sleep disturbances (waking at 3 AM) may relate to cortisol spikes or bladder irritation.

Plan:

Diagnostic Testing:
Order blood tests (via LabCorp/Quest, out-of-pocket): fasting insulin, fasting glucose, A1C, liver enzymes, estradiol, progesterone, and cortisol to assess insulin resistance, liver function, and hormonal status.

Patient to upload recent mycotoxin test and fasting insulin results (December/January).

Consider testing in Kauai during 8-day trip (starting tomorrow) if feasible.

Antimicrobial Therapy:
Prescribe penicillin (link to be provided) to address suspected bacterial overgrowth.

Continue pau d’arco tea (1 qt/day), colloidal silver (2 tsp 3-4x/day), and caprylic acid; monitor for die-off.

Pause probiotics (Dr. Ohhira’s) during penicillin course to avoid interference.

Dietary Modifications:
Gradually introduce low-fiber, easily digestible carbs (e.g., pineapple juice, coconut water, honey) while on penicillin to support metabolism and reduce cortisol stress.

Aim for 60-65% carbs, 15-20% fat, and rest protein; start with small amounts to avoid triggering pain.

Monitor blood sugar and symptoms closely; log food intake and pain onset in Chronometer.

Avoid known triggers (wheat, dairy, high-fiber carbs) during initial phase.

Hormonal Support:
Consider progesterone supplementation post-testing to address potential estrogen dominance and reduce stress response.

Evaluate thyroid function (TSH, free T3, free T4) based on test results, as low thyroid may contribute to fatigue and brain fog.

Symptom Management:
Continue Rife machine for bladder/kidney pain relief as needed.

Maintain magnesium (1400 mg LifeMag + 600 mg Glycinate) to support bowel regularity.

Use detox binders (Pectasol-C, cilantro, HM-ET Binder) cautiously to manage die-off; avoid charcoal if constipating.

Follow-Up:
Schedule follow-up video call in ~10-14 days (post-trip) to review test results and penicillin response.

Adjust carb intake and antimicrobial therapy based on symptom response and labs.

Provide dietary guidance to balance glucose needs with gut tolerance.

Patient Education:
Explain importance of glucose for brain function and metabolic health; low-carb diet may exacerbate cortisol and insulin resistance.

Discuss role of bacterial endotoxins in blocking pancreatic function and glucose uptake.

Encourage tracking of symptoms, carb intake, and pain timing to identify patterns.

Notes:

Patient to travel to Kauai for 8 days; may attempt carb introduction (e.g., coconut water) if penicillin available.

Emphasize gradual carb reintroduction with antimicrobial support to minimize gut fermentation and pain.

Monitor for die-off reactions and adjust antifungal/detox doses as needed.

explore mycotoxin effects

gut microbiome health
submission.daltonsFinalNotes:
for the microbial labs she uploaded - ensure to include this note “PCR detects DNA/RNA fragments, which means it can pick up dead bacteria or organisms present at very low levels that may not be clinically relevant. Atopobium vaginae has a high load, meaning it is likely the most relevant bacteria”

exclude iodine / thyroid

Clostridium Butyricum (Miyairi 588)

MegaSporeBiotic - add in note “Spore probiotics can function like “biological binders.” Their cell walls and metabolites can sequester certain mycotoxins, while also shifting the microbiome toward species that metabolize or neutralize them, reducing systemic absorption and toxicity.”

oregano oil

garlic
